prompt: You get a text from someone you ghosted saying they are in NYC. Do you respond or leave it on read? Be honest.
You find out your closest friend back home has been venting about you to mutual friends. Do you bring it up or let it go?
Someone you have a complicated history with follows you on Instagram. Do you follow back?
You run into your ex on the subway and have three stops together. Do you talk or pretend to be on your phone?
Your best friend from home is visiting NYC for a week and wants to stay with you. Your apartment is a mess and your life is chaotic. Are you saying yes?
If your best friend from home met your best friend here, what is the one thing they would both agree on about you?
What is a compliment someone gave you here that took you a while to realize was actually a compliment?
Think of the person you text the most. Would you have ever been friends with them if you both were back in your home countries?
What is the most lost in translation moment you have had with someone you actually really liked?
If you had to describe your social life here using only food from your home country, what would it look like?
You get offered your dream internship but it pays nothing and you would have to borrow money to survive. Are you taking it?
A professor offers to be your mentor but everyone says they are brutal and demanding. Do you say yes?
You find out a classmate submitted something almost identical to your project and got a better grade. Do you say something?
Someone offers you a job that has nothing to do with your degree but pays well and would let you stay in NYC. Are you taking it?
You have a chance to present your work to a room full of industry people but you feel completely unprepared. Do you go for it or wait until you are ready?
What is something your home country does better professionally that nobody here seems to understand?
If you could swap your major with anyone you know here, who would it be and why?
What is one career path you have considered here purely because it would let you stay?
What is the wildest side hustle you have thought about starting since moving here?
If your LinkedIn had to be 100 percent honest, what would the headline say?
Your parents want to video call every day. You love them but that is a lot. Do you set a boundary or just pick up every time?
A family member makes a comment about your life choices here that genuinely stings. Do you clap back or let it slide?
Your family wants you to come home for a holiday but flights are expensive and you have deadlines. Do you go?
You find out a family member has been telling relatives a very edited version of what your life here looks like. Do you correct the record?
Your parents offer to send you money but there are strings attached. Do you take it?
What is something you do here that you would never tell your parents about, not because it is bad but because it would require too much explaining?
If you had to describe your current living situation to your family using only things they would understand, how would you do it?

What is a piece of advice your parents gave you before you left that turned out to be completely wrong?

You get invited to something completely outside your comfort zone by someone you want to impress. Do you go?

What is the hardest thing you have figured out how to do alone since moving here?
